mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate surpasses 9.0%, corporate earnings expectations deteriorate sharply, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day forward returns in expansion regimes, or the signal loses predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and prompts anticipated monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases consistently coincide with positive 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll decline exceeding 500 thousand workers signals severe labor market contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 500 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in over 55% of triggered months, or Rank IC remains negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and falling federal funds rates create a favorable valuation and earnings environment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns when GDPC1 rises and FEDFUNDS falls, or interaction term significance drops below 10% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth surpassing 2.0% month-over-month confirms robust consumer spending momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in 60% of instances where RSAFS growth exceeds 2.0%, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in walk-forward validation.
